title: easyEWAS Demo Data Full Tutorial
text: Key parameters: chipType: one "27K", "450K", "EPICV1", "EPICV2", "MSA" Input: none Output: annotation file cached locally (used later startEWAS())
code: library(easyEWAS) downloadAnnotEWAS(chipType = "EPICV2")

title: easyEWAS Demo Data Full Tutorial
text: Key parameters: outpath = "default": create EWASresult/ current working directory Input: none Output: memory: res (R6 object), res$outpath disk: <working_directory>/EWASresult/
code: res <- initEWAS(outpath = "default") res$outpath

title: easyEWAS Demo Data Full Tutorial
text: Key parameters: ExpoData = sampledata MethyData = methydata Input: sampledata: sample-level metadata; first column must sample IDs methydata: CpG--sample matrix; first column must probe IDs Output: memory: res$Data$Expo, res$Data$Methy disk: none
code: res <- loadEWAS(   input = res,   ExpoData = sampledata,   MethyData = methydata )  str(res$Data$Expo) dim(res$Data$Methy)

title: easyEWAS Demo Data Full Tutorial
text: Key parameters: Vars: comma-separated variable names res$Data$Expo TypeTo: "factor" "numeric" Input: res$Data$Expo Step 2 Output: memory: selected columns updated res$Data$Expo disk: none
code: res <- transEWAS(   input = res,   Vars = "cov1,var",   TypeTo = "factor" )

title: easyEWAS Demo Data Full Tutorial
text: Key parameters: batch: batch column name sample metadata adjustVar: covariates preserve ComBat plot = TRUE: save prior diagnostic plot Input: res$Data$Methy res$Data$Expo Output: memory: corrected methylation matrix res$Data$Methy disk: combat_plots.pdf (plot = TRUE)
code: res <- batchEWAS(   input = res,   adjustVar = "cov1,cov2",   batch = "batch",   plot = TRUE,   par.prior = TRUE,   mean.only = FALSE,   ref.batch = NULL,   parallel = FALSE )

title: easyEWAS Demo Data Full Tutorial
text: Key parameters: model: "lm", "lmer", "cox" expo: exposure variable cov: comma-separated covariates chipType: annotation platform filename: output CSV name ("default" -> ewasresult.csv) Input: corrected methylation matrix + sample metadata res Output: memory: res$result, res$model, res$formula, res$covdata disk: <outpath>/ewasresult.csv (<filename>.csv)
code: res <- startEWAS(   input = res,   filename = "ewas_lm",   model = "lm",   expo = "var",   cov = "cov1,cov2",   chipType = "EPICV2",   adjustP = TRUE,   core = "default" )  head(res$result)

title: easyEWAS Demo Data Full Tutorial
text: Key parameters: p: p-value column res$result (example "PVAL" "FDR") plot.type: "m" (Manhattan), "q" (QQ), "c" (circular), "d" (density) file file.name: image format prefix Input: res$result annotation columns (chr, pos) Output: memory: res$CMplot disk: plot files res$outpath
code: res <- plotEWAS(   input = res,   p = "PVAL",   threshold = 0.05,   plot.type = c("m", "q"),   file = "jpg",   file.name = "EWAS_plot" )

title: easyEWAS Demo Data Full Tutorial
text: Key parameters: filterP: significance column used select CpGs cutoff: threshold CpG selection times: bootstrap iterations bootCI: confidence interval method ("perc" commonly used) Input: res$result, plus model context res (res$model, res$formula) Output: memory: res$bootres disk: <outpath>/bootresult.csv (custom filename)
code: res <- bootEWAS(   input = res,   filterP = "PVAL",   cutoff = 0.001,   times = 100,   bootCI = "perc",   filename = "boot_lm" )

title: easyEWAS Demo Data Full Tutorial
text: Key parameters: method: "GO" "KEGG" filterP + cutoff: select significant genes EWAS result plot, plotType: whether save dot/bar enrichment figure Input: gene symbols EWAS result column gene Output: memory: res$enrichres disk: enrichment CSV + optional PDF plot
code: res <- enrichEWAS(   input = res,   method = "GO",   ont = "BP",   filterP = "PVAL",   cutoff = 0.05,   pAdjustMethod = "BH",   plot = TRUE,   plotType = "dot",   plotcolor = "p.adjust",   showCategory = 10,   filename = "go_enrich" )

title: easyEWAS Demo Data Full Tutorial
text: Key parameters: chipType genome must match platform (EPICV2 use hg38) epicv2Filter: EPICv2 replicate probe handling strategy lambda, C, min.cpgs: region calling sensitivity Input: res$Data$Methy + res$Data$Expo Output: memory: res$dmrres disk: <outpath>/DMRresult.csv (custom filename)
code: BiocManager::install("DMRcate")  res <- dmrEWAS(   input = res,   chipType = "EPICV2",   what = "Beta",   epicv2Filter = "mean",   expo = "var",   cov = "cov1,cov2",   genome = "hg38",   fdrCPG = 0.05,   pcutoff = "fdr",   lambda = 1000,   C = 2,   min.cpgs = 2,   filename = "dmr_result" )

title: easyEWAS Demo Data Full Tutorial
text: pipeline, check common outputs res$outpath: ewas_lm.csv (ewasresult.csv) combat_plots.pdf (batch step plot = TRUE) EWAS plot image files plotEWAS() boot_lm.csv (bootresult.csv) go_enrich.csv go_enrich.pdf (enrichment plot enabled) dmr_result.csv (DMR analysis run)

title: easyEWAS Quickstart
code: install.packages(c("remotes", "BiocManager")) remotes::install_github("ytwangZero/easyEWAS")

title: easyEWAS Quickstart
code: library(easyEWAS)

title: easyEWAS Quickstart
text: startEWAS() needs chip annotation data. Download cache analysis. host annotation files server:
code: downloadAnnotEWAS(chipType = "EPICV2") options(   easyEWAS.annotation_base_url =     "https://your-server/path/to/annotation" ) downloadAnnotEWAS(chipType = "EPICV2")

title: easyEWAS Quickstart
code: res <- initEWAS(outpath = "default")  res <- loadEWAS(   input = res,   ExpoData = sampledata,   MethyData = methydata )  res <- transEWAS(   input = res,   Vars = "cov1",   TypeTo = "factor" )  res <- startEWAS(   input = res,   model = "lm",   expo = "var",   cov = "cov1,cov2",   chipType = "EPICV2" )

title: easyEWAS Quickstart
code: res <- plotEWAS(   input = res,   p = "PVAL",   threshold = 0.05,   file = "jpg" )

title: easyEWAS Quickstart
text: DMR analysis, install DMRcate first:
code: res <- bootEWAS(   input = res,   filterP = "PVAL",   cutoff = 0.001,   times = 100 )  res <- enrichEWAS(   input = res,   method = "GO",   filterP = "PVAL",   cutoff = 0.05 ) BiocManager::install("DMRcate")  res <- dmrEWAS(   input = res,   chipType = "EPICV2",   what = "Beta",   expo = "var",   cov = "cov1,cov2",   genome = "hg38" )

title: easyEWAS Quickstart
text: outputs written folder created initEWAS(), default: EWASresult/ewasresult.csv Plot files plotEWAS() Optional downstream result files (bootresult.csv, enrichresult.csv, DMRresult.csv)

title: easyEWAS Troubleshooting Guide
code: sessionInfo() packageVersion("easyEWAS") str(res$Data$Expo) str(res$Data$Methy)

title: easyEWAS Troubleshooting Guide
code: install.packages(c("remotes", "BiocManager")) BiocManager::install(c("sva", "clusterProfiler", "org.Hs.eg.db", "DMRcate")) remotes::install_github("ytwangZero/easyEWAS")

title: easyEWAS Troubleshooting Guide
code: BiocManager::install("DMRcate")

title: easyEWAS Troubleshooting Guide
code: downloadAnnotEWAS(chipType = "EPICV2")

title: easyEWAS Troubleshooting Guide
code: options(easyEWAS.annotation_base_url = "https://your-server/path/to/annotation") downloadAnnotEWAS(chipType = "EPICV2")

title: easyEWAS Troubleshooting Guide
text: Rules: ExpoData first column: sample IDs MethyData first column: probe IDs MethyData sample columns must match ExpoData sample IDs

title: easyEWAS Troubleshooting Guide
text: Use : "lm" "lmer" "cox"

title: easyEWAS Troubleshooting Guide
text: Convert numeric:
code: res$Data$Expo$status <- as.numeric(as.character(res$Data$Expo$status))

title: easyEWAS Troubleshooting Guide
code: res <- transEWAS(res, Vars = "var", TypeTo = "factor")

title: easyEWAS Troubleshooting Guide
code: colnames(res$Data$Expo)

title: easyEWAS Troubleshooting Guide
text: Try: plot = FALSE check NA patterns verify batch enough samples

title: easyEWAS Troubleshooting Guide
text: Run startEWAS() plotEWAS().

title: easyEWAS Troubleshooting Guide
code: colnames(res$result)

title: easyEWAS Troubleshooting Guide
text: Relax cutoff:
code: res <- bootEWAS(res, filterP = "PVAL", cutoff = 0.01, times = 100)

title: easyEWAS Troubleshooting Guide
text: Check significance threshold gene mapping quality.

title: easyEWAS Troubleshooting Guide
text: Recommended genome/chip combinations: EPICV2 + hg38 EPICV1 / 450K + hg19

title: Authors and Citation
text: Yuting Wang. Author, maintainer. Xu Gao. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Wang Y, Gao X (2026). easyEWAS: Perform Visualize EWAS Analysis. R package version 1.1.0, https://ytwangZero.github.io/easyEWAS/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {easyEWAS: Perform and Visualize EWAS Analysis},   author = {Yuting Wang and Xu Gao},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.1.0},   url = {https://ytwangZero.github.io/easyEWAS/}, }

title: easyEWAS
text: Epigenome-Wide Association Study R easyEWAS R package conducting Epigenome-Wide Association Study (EWAS) unified reproducible way. supports Illumina methylation array platforms including 27K, 450K, EPIC v1, EPIC v2, MSA, provides end--end analysis workflow covering association modeling, batch-effect handling, result visualization, bootstrap-based internal validation, enrichment analysis, optional DMR discovery.

title: easyEWAS
text: Install GitHub: Load package:
code: remotes::install_github("ytwangZero/easyEWAS") library(easyEWAS)

title: easyEWAS
text: dmrEWAS() depends DMRcate, Suggests installed automatically minimal setups.
code: BiocManager::install("DMRcate")

title: easyEWAS
text: Large chip annotation tables now downloaded demand cached locally, instead bundled inside main package tarball. makes installation lighter, faster, stable GitHub users. Download annotation (one-time per chip type): needed, set custom annotation host:
code: downloadAnnotEWAS(chipType = "EPICV2") options(easyEWAS.annotation_base_url = "https://github.com/ytwangZero/easyEWAS_materials/raw/main/annotation")

title: easyEWAS
text: Wang Y, Jiang M, Niu S, Gao X. easyEWAS: flexible user-friendly R package epigenome-wide association study[J]. Bioinformatics Advances, 2025, 5(1): vbaf026.

title: Perform batch effect correction — batchEWAS
text: Perform batch effect correction based function ComBat form R package sva. requires "batches" data set known. uses either parametric non-parametric empirical Bayes frameworks adjusting data batch effects.

title: Perform batch effect correction — batchEWAS
code: batchEWAS(   input,   adjustVar = NULL,   batch = NULL,   plot = FALSE,   par.prior = TRUE,   mean.only = FALSE,   ref.batch = NULL,   parallel = FALSE,   core = NULL )

title: Perform batch effect correction — batchEWAS
text: input R6 class integrated information. adjustVar (Optional) Names variate interest covariates besides batch, name separated comma. Ensure correcting batch effects, effects factors appropriately considered adjusted .Ensure space. e.g. "cov1,cov2". batch Name batch variable. plot Logical. TRUE give prior plots black kernel estimate empirical batch effect density red parametric. Thr default FALSE. par.prior Logical. TRUE indicates parametric adjustments used, FALSE indicates non-parametric adjustments used. mean.Logical. Default FALSE. TRUE, ComBat corrects mean batch effect (scale adjustment). ref.batch (Optional) NULL given, use selected batch reference batch adjustment. parallel Logical. Whether enable parallel computing batch effect correction. Default FALSE. core Integer. Number CPU cores use parallel = TRUE. Default NULL.

title: Perform batch effect correction — batchEWAS
text: input, R6 class object integrating information.

title: Perform batch effect correction — batchEWAS
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- initEWAS(outpath = "default") res <- loadEWAS(input = res, ExpoData = "default", MethyData = "default") res <- transEWAS(input = res, Vars = "cov1", TypeTo = "factor") res <- batchEWAS(input = res, batch = "batch", par.prior=TRUE, ref.batch = NULL) } # }

title: Perform Bootstrap-based Internal Validation — bootEWAS
text: Users can perform internal validation identified differently methylated sites based bootstrap method.

title: Perform Bootstrap-based Internal Validation — bootEWAS
code: bootEWAS(input, filterP = "PVAL", cutoff = 0.05, CpGs = NULL, times = 500, bootCI = "perc",filename = "default")

title: Perform Bootstrap-based Internal Validation — bootEWAS
text: input R6 class integrated information obtained startEWAS plotEWAS function. filterP name p value columns "PVAL", "FDR", "Bonfferoni." Users use P-value screen significance sites conduct internal validation. cutoff cutoff value P-value used filter internal validation. default 0.05. CpGs name methylation site specified user bootstrap analysis, separated commas. careful spaces, "cpg1,cpg2". times Number bootstrap times specified user. default value 100 times. bootCI vector character strings representing type interval base test . value one "norm", "basic", "stud", "perc" (default), "bca". filename User-customized .csv file name storing bootstrap results. "default", named "bootresult".

title: Perform Bootstrap-based Internal Validation — bootEWAS
text: input, R6 class object integrating information.

title: Perform Bootstrap-based Internal Validation — bootEWAS
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- initEWAS(outpath = "default") res <- loadEWAS(input = res, ExpoData = "default", MethyData = "default") res <- transEWAS(input = res, Vars = "cov1", TypeTo = "factor") res <- startEWAS(input = res, chipType = "EPICV2", model = "lm", expo = "default", adjustP = TRUE) res <- bootEWAS(input = res, filterP = "PVAL", cutoff = 0.05, times = 100) } # }

what: Perform Differentially Methylated Region analysis — dmrEWAS
title: Perform Differentially Methylated Region analysis — dmrEWAS
text: Perform differential methylation analysis based R package DMRcate. Computes kernel estimate null comparison identify significantly DMRs.

title: Perform Differentially Methylated Region analysis — dmrEWAS
code: dmrEWAS(   input,   chipType = "EPICV2",   what = "Beta",   epicv2Filter = "mean",   expo = NULL,   cov = NULL,   genome = "hg38",   fdrCPG = 0.05,   pcutoff = "fdr",   lambda = 1000,   C = 2,   min.cpgs = 2,   filename = "default" )

title: Perform Differentially Methylated Region analysis — dmrEWAS
text: input R6 class integrated information. chipType Illumina chip versions user measurement methylation data, including "450K","EPICV1", "EPICV2". default "EPICV2". Types methylation values, including "Beta" "M". Default "Beta". epicv2Filter Strategy filtering probe replicates map CpG site. "mean" takes mean available probes; "sensitivity" takes available probe sensitive methylation change; "precision" either selects available probe lowest variation consensus value (precise), takes mean confers lowest variation instead, "random" takes single probe random replicate group. expo Name exposure variable used DMR analysis. cov Name(s) covariate(s) used DMR analysis,  name separated comma. Ensure space. e.g. "cov1,cov2,cov3". genome Reference genome annotating DMRs. Must consistent array platform used: Use "hg38" EPICV2 arrays. Use "hg19" 450K EPICV1 arrays. Note: genome argument currently affect internal behavior extractRanges() (.e., liftover performed). fdrCPG Used individually assess significance CpG site. FDR-adjusted p-value CpG site specified fdrCPG threshold, site marked significant. default value 0.05. pcutoff Used determine threshold DMRs. strongly recommended use default (fdr), unless confident risk Type errors (false positives). lambda distance two significant CpG sites greater equal lambda, considered belonging different DMRs. default value 1000 nucleotides, meaning distance two significant CpG sites exceeds 1000 nucleotides, separated different DMRs. C Scaling factor bandwidth. Gaussian kernel calculated lambda/C = sigma. Empirical testing shows Illumina bisulfite sequencing data , lambda=1000, near-optimal prediction sequencing-derived DMRs obtained C approximately 2, .e. 1 standard deviation Gaussian kernel = 500 base pairs. < 0.2. min.cpgs Minimum number consecutive CpGs constituting DMR. Default 2. filename User-customized .csv file name storing DMR results. "default", named "DMRresult".

title: Perform Differentially Methylated Region analysis — dmrEWAS
text: input, R6 class object integrating information.

title: Perform Differentially Methylated Region analysis — dmrEWAS
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- initEWAS(outpath = "default") res <- loadEWAS(input = res, ExpoData = "default", MethyData = "default") res <- transEWAS(input = res, Vars = "cov1", TypeTo = "factor") res <- dmrEWAS(input = res, filename = "default", chipType = "EPICV2", what = "Beta", expo = "var", cov = "cov1,cov2", genome = "hg38",) } # }

what: Download and cache chip annotation tables — downloadAnnotEWAS
title: Download and cache chip annotation tables — downloadAnnotEWAS
text: Download CpG annotation tables supported Illumina chip types store local cache directory. downloaded files saved .rds reused later analyses.

title: Download and cache chip annotation tables — downloadAnnotEWAS
code: downloadAnnotEWAS(chipType = c("EPICV2", "EPICV1", "450K", "27K", "MSA"), cache_dir = NULL, force = FALSE, base_url = getOption("easyEWAS.annotation_base_url", "https://github.com/ytwangZero/easyEWAS_materials/raw/main/annotation"), quiet = FALSE)

title: Download and cache chip annotation tables — downloadAnnotEWAS
text: chipType One chip types. Supported values: "EPICV2", "EPICV1", "450K", "27K", "MSA". cache_dir Directory store downloaded annotation files. NULL, default cache path tools::R_user_dir("easyEWAS", "cache")/annotation used. force Logical. TRUE, re-download overwrite existing cached files. base_url Base URL annotation .rds files hosted. quiet Logical. Passed utils::download.file().

title: Download and cache chip annotation tables — downloadAnnotEWAS
text: named character vector local file paths (invisibly).

title: easyEWAS package — easyEWAS-package
text: Tools conducting Epigenome-Wide Association Study (EWAS) visualizing results. Users provide sample metadata methylation matrices run EWAS linear models, linear mixed-effects models, Cox models. package supports downstream visualization, bootstrap validation, enrichment analysis, DMR analysis optional parallel computing.

title: easyEWAS package — easyEWAS-package
text: Maintainer: Yuting Wang ytwang@pku.edu.cn Authors: Xu Gao xu.gao@pku.edu.cn

title: Enrichment analyses — enrichEWAS
text: Perform GO KEGG enrichment analysis based clusterProfiler package.

title: Enrichment analyses — enrichEWAS
code: enrichEWAS(   input,   filename = "default",   method = "GO",   filterP = "PVAL",   cutoff = 0.05,   ont = "BP",   pool = FALSE,   plot = TRUE,   plotType = "dot",   plotcolor = "p.adjust",   x = "GeneRatio",   showCategory = 10,   width = 11,   height = 7,   pvalueCutoff = 0.05,   pAdjustMethod = "BH",   qvalueCutoff = 0.2 )

title: Enrichment analyses — enrichEWAS
text: input R6 class integrated information obtained startEWAS plotEWAS bootEWAS function. filename User-customized .xlsx file name storing EWAS results. "default" chosen, named "enrichresult". method Methods enrichment analysis, including "GO" "KEGG". filterP name p value columns "PVAL", "FDR", "Bonfferoni." Users use P-value screen significance sites conduct enrichment analysis. cutoff cutoff value P-value used filter enrichment analysis. default 0.05. ont choosing GO enrichment analysis, select GO sub-ontology enrichment analysis performed. One "BP", "MF", "CC" sub-ontologies, "" three. Default "BP". pool ont='', whether pool three GO sub-ontologies. plot Whether results enrichment analysis need visualized, default TRUE plotType Whether draw bar plot ("bar") dot plot ("dot"), default "dot". plotcolor vertical axis picture enrichment analysis results. Users can choose "pvalue" "p.adjust" "qvalue". default "p.adjust". x Character string specifying variable used x-axis plot. Common options "GeneRatio" "Count". "GeneRatio": ratio input genes annotated given term. "Count": number input genes annotated term. showCategory number categories displayed plots. Default 10. width Width PDF output inches. Default 11. height Height PDF output inches. Default 7. pvalueCutoff p-value threshold used filter enrichment results. results pass p-value test (.e., smaller value) reported. value refers p-value adjustment. p-value represents probability observing current level enrichment assumption enrichment. smaller p-value, significant enrichment result. pAdjustMethod p-value adjustment method used multiple hypothesis testing, aimed reducing false positives caused multiple comparisons. One "holm", "hochberg", "hommel", "bonferroni", "BH", "", "fdr", "none". qvalueCutoff qvalue cutoff enrichment tests report significant. q-value result controlling false discovery rate (FDR) represents proportion false positives may occur conducting multiple tests.Tests must pass ) pvalueCutoff unadjusted pvalues, ii) pvalueCutoff adjusted pvalues iii) qvalueCutoff qvalues reported. default 0.2.

title: Enrichment analyses — enrichEWAS
text: input, R6 class object integrating information.

title: Enrichment analyses — enrichEWAS
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- initEWAS(outpath = "default") res <- loadEWAS(input = res, ExpoData = "default", MethyData = "default") res <- transEWAS(input = res, Vars = "cov1", TypeTo = "factor") res <- startEWAS(input = res, chipType = "EPICV2", model = "lm", expo = "default", adjustP = TRUE) res <- plotEWAS(input = res, pval = "PVAL") res <- bootEWAS(input = res, filterP = "PVAL", cutoff = 0.05, times = 100) res <- enrichEWAS(input = res, method = "GO", filterP = "PVAL", cutoff = 0.05, pAdjustMethod = "BH") } # }

title: EWAS Model Computation Utilities — ewasfun_lm
text: Model fitting functions EWAS analysis using linear, mixed, Cox models. designed used inside parallel loops minimal memory footprint.

title: EWAS Model Computation Utilities — ewasfun_lm
code: ewasfun_lm(cg, ff, cov, facnum)  ewasfun_lmer(cg, ff, cov, facnum)  ewasfun_cox(cg, ff, cov)

title: EWAS Model Computation Utilities — ewasfun_lm
text: cg row vector representing one CpG site's beta values across samples. ff model formula object (e.g., cpg ~ var1 + var2). cov data.frame containing covariates model. facnum (lm/lmer) Number factor levels exposure variable.

title: EWAS Model Computation Utilities — ewasfun_lm
text: numeric vector model coefficients, standard errors, p-values.

title: Initialize the EWAS module — initEWAS
text: function designed generate R6 class storing data results EWAS analysis, also create folder local computer storing analysis results.

title: Initialize the EWAS module — initEWAS
code: initEWAS(outpath = "default")

title: Initialize the EWAS module — initEWAS
text: outpath user-specified path used store generated folder named "EWASresult" contains analysis results. "default" specified, folder generated current working directory.

title: Initialize the EWAS module — initEWAS
text: input, R6 class object integrating information.

title: Initialize the EWAS module — initEWAS
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- initEWAS(outpath = "default") } # }

what: Load all data files for EWAS module — loadEWAS
title: Load all data files for EWAS module — loadEWAS
text: Upload sample data methylation data EWAS analysis.

title: Load all data files for EWAS module — loadEWAS
code: loadEWAS(input, ExpoPath = NULL, MethyPath = NULL, ExpoData = "default", MethyData = "default")

title: Load all data files for EWAS module — loadEWAS
text: input R6 class integrated information obtained initEWAS function. ExpoPath path store user's sample data. row represents sample, column represents variable (exposure variable covariate). .csv .xlsx file types supported. first column must sample ID, must consistent IDs methylation data. MethyPath path store user's methylation data. row represents CpG site, column represents sample. .csv .xlsx file types supported. first column must CpG probes. sample IDs must consistent IDs sample data. ExpoData data.frame user-supplied sample data loaded R environment. default, example data inside package used. first column must sample name. MethyData data.frame user-supplied methylation data loaded R environment. default, example methylation data inside package loaded. first column must CpG site name.

title: Load all data files for EWAS module — loadEWAS
text: input, R6 class object integrating information.

title: Load all data files for EWAS module — loadEWAS
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- initEWAS(outpath = "default") res <- loadEWAS(input = res, ExpoData = "default", MethyData = "default") } # }

code: methydata

what: Visualize the results of EWAS analysis — plotEWAS
title: Visualize the results of EWAS analysis — plotEWAS
text: Visualize EWAS results based CMplot package, including Manhattan plots, QQ plots, etc. Please note function supports plotting single-layer circular Manhattan plot. Additionally, meaning parameter function exactly CMplot detailed information create multi-layer circular Manhattan plots, please refer CMplot (https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/CMplot/index.html).

title: Visualize the results of EWAS analysis — plotEWAS
code: plotEWAS(   input,   p = "PVAL",   threshold = NULL,   file = c("jpg", "pdf", "tiff", "png"),   col = c("#4197d8", "#f8c120", "#413496", "#495226", "#d60b6f", "#e66519", "#d581b7",     "#83d3ad", "#7c162c", "#26755d"),   bin.size = 1e+06,   bin.breaks = NULL,   LOG10 = TRUE,   pch = 19,   type = "p",   band = 1,   H = 1.5,   ylim = NULL,   axis.cex = 1,   axis.lwd = 1.5,   lab.cex = 1.5,   lab.font = 2,   plot.type = c("m", "c", "q", "d"),   multracks = FALSE,   multracks.xaxis = FALSE,   multraits = FALSE,   points.alpha = 100L,   r = 0.3,   cex = c(0.5, 1, 1),   outward = FALSE,   ylab = expression(-log[10](italic(p))),   ylab.pos = 3,   xticks.pos = 1,   mar = c(3, 6, 3, 3),   mar.between = 0,   threshold.col = "red",   threshold.lwd = 1,   threshold.lty = 2,   amplify = FALSE,   signal.cex = 1.5,   signal.pch = 19,   signal.col = NULL,   signal.line = 2,   highlight = NULL,   highlight.cex = 1,   highlight.pch = 19,   highlight.type = "p",   highlight.col = "red",   highlight.text = NULL,   highlight.text.col = "black",   highlight.text.cex = 1,   highlight.text.font = 3,   chr.labels = NULL,   chr.border = FALSE,   chr.labels.angle = 0,   chr.den.col = "black",   chr.pos.max = FALSE,   cir.band = 1,   cir.chr = TRUE,   cir.chr.h = 1.5,   cir.axis = TRUE,   cir.axis.col = "black",   cir.axis.grid = TRUE,   conf.int = TRUE,   conf.int.col = NULL,   file.output = TRUE,   file.name = "",   dpi = 300,   height = NULL,   width = NULL,   main = "",   main.cex = 1.5,   main.font = 2,   legend.ncol = NULL,   legend.cex = 1,   legend.pos = c("left", "middle", "right"),   box = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Visualize the results of EWAS analysis — plotEWAS
text: input R6 class integrated information obtained startEWAS function. p user needs specify name p value selected result visualization. threshold significant threshold.threshold = 0 NULL, threshold line added. file format output image file, including "jpg","pdf","tiff", "png". col vector specifies colors chromosomes. length col shorter number chromosomes, colors applied cyclically. bin.size integer, size bin bp marker density plot. bin.breaks vector, set breaks legend density plot, e.g., seq(min, max, step), windows number markers range plotted colors min max value. LOG10 logical, whether change p-value log10(p-value) scale. pch integer, shape points, "pch" plot. type character, "p" (point), "l" (cross line), "h" (vertical lines) , "type" plot. band number, size space chromosomes, default 1. H number controlling height circular Manhattan track. ylim vector (c(min, max)), CMplot plot points among interval. axis.cex number, controls size ticks labels X/Y-axis ticks labels axis circle plot. axis.lwd number, controls thickness X/Y-axis lines thickness axis circle plot. lab.cex number, controls size labels X/Y-axis labels chromosomes circle plot. lab.font number, controls font labels axis. plot.type character vector, "d", "c", "m", "q" can used. plot.type="d", CpG density plotted; plot.type="c", circle-Manhattan plot plotted; plot.type="m",Manhattan plot plotted; plot.type="q",Q-Q plot plotted; plot.type=c("m","q"), Manhattan Q-Q plots plotted. multracks Logical. Whether use multi-track mode CMplot. multracks.xaxis Logical. Whether draw x-axis multi-track mode. multraits Logical. Whether use multi-trait mode CMplot. points.alpha Integer. Transparency (alpha) value points (0–255). Default 100. r number, radius circle (inside radius), default 1. cex number vector, size points, "size" plot, vector, first number controls size points circle plot(default 0.5), second number controls size points Manhattan plot (default 1), third number controls size points Q-Q plot (default 1) outward logical, TRUE, points plotted inside outside circular Manhattan plot. ylab character, labels y axis. ylab.pos distance ylab yaxis. xticks.pos distance labels x ticks x axis. mar size white gaps around plot, 4 values provided, indicating direction bottom, left, , right. mar.Space tracks multi-track plotting. threshold.col character vector, color line threshold levels, can also control color diagonal line QQplot. threshold.lwd number vector, width line threshold levels, can also control thickness diagonal line QQplot. threshold.lty number vector, type line threshold levels, can also control type diagonal line QQplot amplify logical, CMplot can amplify significant points, TRUE, points bigger minimal significant level amplified, default: amplify=TRUE. signal.cex number, amplify=TRUE, users can set size significant points. signal.pch number, amplify=TRUE, users can set shape significant points. signal.col character, amplify=TRUE, users can set colour significant points, signal.col=NULL, colors significant points changed. signal.line number, thickness lines significant CpGs cross circle. highlight vector, names CpGs need highlighted. highlight.cex vector, size points CpGs need highlighted. highlight.pch vector, pch points CpGs need highlighted. highlight.type vector, type points CpGs need highlighted. highlight.col vector, col points CpGs need highlighted. highlight.text vector, text added around highlighted CpGs. highlight.text.col vector, color added text. highlight.text.cex value, size added text. highlight.text.font text font highlighted CpGs chr.labels vector, labels chromosomes density plot Manhattan plot. chr.border logical, whether plot dot line chromosomes. chr.labels.angle value, rotate tick labels x-axis Manhattan plot (-90 < chr.labels.angle < 90). chr.den.col character vector NULL, colour CpG density. length parameter 'chr.den.col' bigger 1, CpG density counts number CpG within given size ('bin.size') plotted around circle. chr.den.col=NULL, density bar attached bottom manhattan plot. chr.pos.max logical, whether physical positions chromosome contain maximum length chromosome. cir.band number controlling spacing circular tracks. cir.chr logical, boundary represents chromosomes plotted periphery circle, default TRUE. cir.chr.h number, width boundary, cir.chr=FALSE, parameter useless. cir.axis logical, whether add axis circle Manhattan plot. cir.axis.col character, color axis circle. cir.axis.grid logical, whether add axis grid line circles. conf.int logical, whether plot confidence interval QQ-plot. conf.int.col character vector, color confidence interval QQplot. file.output logical, users can choose whether output plot results. file.name character vector, names output files. dpi number, picture resolution '.jpg', '.npg', '.tiff' files. default 300. height height output files. width width output files. main character vector, title plot manhattan plot qqplot. main.cex size title. main.font font title. legend.ncol Number columns used legend. legend.cex numeric value controlling legend text size. legend.pos character value specifying legend position. box logical, function draws box around current plot. verbose whether print log information.

title: Visualize the results of EWAS analysis — plotEWAS
text: updated input object, including CMplot-ready data stored input$CMplot.

title: Visualize the results of EWAS analysis — plotEWAS
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- initEWAS(outpath = "default") res <- loadEWAS(input = res, ExpoData = "default", MethyData = "default") res <- transEWAS(input = res, Vars = "cov1", TypeTo = "factor") res <- startEWAS(input = res, chipType = "EPICV2", model = "lm", expo = "var", adjustP = TRUE) res <- plotEWAS(input = res, p = "PVAL") } # }

title: Perform EWAS Analysis — startEWAS
text: Perform EWAS analysis obtain coefficient value, standard deviation significance p value (adjust p value) site.

title: Perform EWAS Analysis — startEWAS
code: startEWAS(   input,   filename = "default",   model = "lm",   expo = NULL,   cov = NULL,   random = NULL,   time = NULL,   status = NULL,   adjustP = TRUE,   chipType = "EPICV2",   core = "default",   annotation_cache = NULL,   auto_download_annotation = FALSE,   annotation_base_url = getOption("easyEWAS.annotation_base_url",     "https://github.com/ytwangZero/easyEWAS_materials/raw/main/annotation") )

title: Perform EWAS Analysis — startEWAS
text: input R6 class integrated information obtained loadEWAS transEWAS function. filename filename Name output CSV file store EWAS results. set "default", file named "ewasresult.csv" saved specified output directory. model Statistical model use EWAS analysis. Options include: "lm": Linear regression (default) "lmer": Linear mixed-effects model "cox": Cox proportional hazards model expo Name exposure variable used EWAS analysis. cov Comma-separated list covariate variable names include model (e.g., "age,sex,bmi"). include spaces names. Optional. random Name grouping variable random intercept, required using "lmer" model. time Name time--event variable, required using "cox" model. status Name event/censoring indicator variable, required using "cox" model. adjustP Logical. TRUE (default), adjusts p-values using FDR (Benjamini-Hochberg) Bonferroni correction methods. chipType Illumina array platform used DNA methylation measurement. Available options: "27K" "450K" "EPICV1" "EPICV2" (default) "MSA" core Number CPU cores use parallel processing. set "default", uses number available physical cores minus one. annotation_cache Local directory cached annotation files. NULL, uses tools::R_user_dir("easyEWAS", "cache")/annotation. auto_download_annotation Logical. TRUE, annotation files downloaded automatically missing. annotation_base_url Base URL annotation .rds files.

title: Perform EWAS Analysis — startEWAS
text: input, R6 class object integrating information.

title: Perform EWAS Analysis — startEWAS
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- initEWAS(outpath = "default") res <- loadEWAS(input = res, ExpoData = "default", MethyData = "default") res <- transEWAS(input = res, Vars = "cov1", TypeTo = "factor") downloadAnnotEWAS(chipType = "EPICV2") res <- startEWAS(input = res, filename = "default", chipType = "EPICV2", model = "lm", expo = "var", cov = "cov1,cov2",adjustP = TRUE, core = "default") } # }
